description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, symmetrical design. The axe features two large, curved blades with pointed tips, each extending outward from a central mounting. The handle is cylindrical, straight, and appears to be made of wood, reinforced with several dark metal bands for added grip and durability. The bottom of the handle is capped with a metal ring, possibly for hanging or attaching a lanyard. The overall finish is matte, with the blades showing a gradient from dark to lighter metal, suggesting wear or intentional coloration. The construction and style evoke a medieval or fantasy aesthetic, suitable for display or theatrical use in an urban context such as a themed shop, garage, or park installation.
description: A double-bladed battle axe with a wooden handle reinforced by metal bands and a matte, dark-finished blade. The design is symmetrical and robust, with a metal ring at the handle's base.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large, curved blades and the full width of the axe head.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the symmetrical double blades.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and straight handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, reinforced with dark metal bands, no visible curvature, with a metal ring at the base for grip or attachment.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical, wide curved blades with pointed tips and a central mounting; no beard or poll, matte finish with gradient coloration.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible metal bands securing the blade to the handle.
